99:pub struct LuaTable {

229:pub struct LuaTable {
254:impl LuaTable {

225	///
226	/// When a table has no hash part, `self.node` is empty and
227	/// `self.lastfree` is `None`.  This replaces the C idiom of storing a
228	/// pointer to a shared static `dummynode_` and checking `lastfree == NULL`.
229	pub struct LuaTable {
230	    /// Bit-packed: lower bits = absent-metamethod flags; bit 7 = BIT_RAS.
231	    /// C: `lu_byte flags`.
232	    pub flags: TableFlags,
233	    /// `log2` of the hash-part node count.  `1u32 << lsizenode` = capacity.
234	    /// C: `lu_byte lsizenode`.
235	    pub lsizenode: u8,
236	    /// Array-part size hint or real size depending on `flags.is_real_asize()`.
237	    /// C: `unsigned int alimit`.
238	    pub alimit: u32,
239	    /// The array part; 0-based in Rust (Lua index `k` → `self.array[k-1]`).
240	    /// C: `TValue *array`.
241	    pub array: Vec<LuaValue>,
242	    /// The hash part; length is `1 << lsizenode` when not dummy, 0 when dummy.
243	    /// C: `Node *node`.
244	    pub node: Vec<TableNode>,
245	    /// Cursor into `self.node` for free-slot scanning (one-past the last tried
246	    /// position).  `None` when using the dummy hash part.
247	    /// C: `Node *lastfree` (NULL = dummy).
248	    pub lastfree: Option<usize>,
249	    /// Optional metatable.
250	    /// C: `struct Table *metatable`.
251	    pub metatable: Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
252	}
253	
254	impl LuaTable {
255	    // ── Predicate helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
256	
257	    /// `isdummy(t)` — true when the table has no allocated hash part.
258	    #[inline]
259	    pub fn is_dummy(&self) -> bool {
260	        self.lastfree.is_none()
261	    }
262	
263	    /// `sizenode(t)` — nominal hash-part capacity (`1 << lsizenode`).
264	    /// This is the modulo used for hash indexing, not the vec length.
265	    #[inline]
266	    fn sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
267	        1u32 << self.lsizenode
268	    }
269	
270	    /// `allocsizenode(t)` — 0 when dummy, else `1 << lsizenode`.
271	    #[inline]
272	    pub fn alloc_sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
273	        if self.is_dummy() { 0 } else { self.sizenode() }
274	    }
275	
276	    /// `isrealasize(t)` accessor; delegates to `TableFlags`.
277	    #[inline]
278	    fn is_real_asize(&self) -> bool { self.flags.is_real_asize() }
279	
280	    // ── ispow2 (C macro with 0-as-pow2 semantics) ─────────────────────────
281	
282	    /// `ispow2(x)` — C treats 0 as a power of two (`(x & (x-1)) == 0`).
283	    #[inline]
284	    fn is_pow2(x: u32) -> bool {
No matches found

1	//! Lua table implementation (array + hash hybrid).
2	//!
3	//! Port of `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/ltable.c` (995 lines, 28 functions).
4	//! The companion header `ltable.h` is merged here per PORTING.md §1.
5	//!
6	//! # Design overview
7	//!
8	//! Tables keep elements in two parts: an **array part** (`Vec<LuaValue>`,
9	//! 1-based indices 1..=alimit) and a **hash part** (`Vec<TableNode>`,
10	//! sized to a power-of-two).  Non-negative integer keys are candidates for
11	//! the array part; all other keys go into the hash part.
12	//!
13	//! The hash part uses Brent's variation of chained scatter tables.  The key
14	//! invariant: if an element is not in its *main position* (the slot its hash
15	//! maps to), then the colliding element *is* in its own main position.
16	//!
17	//! ## C → Rust structural differences
18	//!
19	//! | C pattern | Rust pattern |
20	//! |---|---|
21	//! | `Node *` (pointer into hash array) | `usize` index into `self.node` |
22	//! | `const TValue *` return from get functions | `TableSlotRef` enum |
23	//! | `&absentkey` sentinel | `TableSlotRef::Absent` |
24	//! | `lastfree == NULL` (dummy) | `lastfree: None` |
25	//! | `dummynode` static | empty `node: vec![]` + `lastfree: None` |
26	//! | `nodefromval(v)` pointer subtraction | index stored in `TableSlotRef::Hash(i)` |
27	//!
28	//! ## `TableSlotRef`
29	//!
30	//! C functions like `luaH_getint` return `const TValue *`, which may point to
31	//! an array slot, a hash-node value, or the static `absentkey` sentinel.
32	//! Rust can't model this as a borrow that spans the C-style "get then set"
33	//! two-phase pattern, so we use an explicit `TableSlotRef` that carries an
34	//! index into either the array or the node vector.  Phase B will revisit
35	//! whether a `&LuaValue` return is feasible with lifetime annotations.
36	//!
37	//! # C source files
38	//! - `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/ltable.c`  (995 lines, 28 functions)
39	//! - `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/ltable.h`  (63 lines; merged here)
40	
41	// C: #define ltable_c
42	// C: #define LUA_CORE
43	
44	use std::rc::Rc;
45	#[allow(unused_imports)] use crate::prelude::*;
46	
47	// TODO(port): import paths will stabilize in Phase B once the crate graph is wired.
48	use crate::object::ceil_log2;
49	use crate::state::{GcRef, LuaState, LuaValue, LuaClosure};
50	use lua_types::LuaString;
51	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
52	use lua_types::StackIdx;
53	
54	// ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55	
56	/// Largest `k` such that `2^k` fits in a signed `i32` (`sizeof(int)*8 - 1`).
57	/// C: `#define MAXABITS cast_int(sizeof(int) * CHAR_BIT - 1)`
58	const MAXABITS: u32 = (std::mem::size_of::<i32>() as u32) * 8 - 1; // = 31
59	
60	/// Maximum size of the array part: `min(2^MAXABITS, usize::MAX / sizeof(LuaValue))`.

# type-vocabulary.tsv — canonical owners for cross-crate Rust types.
#
# Format:
#   <name> <kind> <owner-path> <mode> <notes>
#
# mode:
#   enforce  hook fails if a modified file defines this name outside owner-path
#   audit    audit reports it, but changed-file hook does not fail
#
# Keep this list small and intentional. It is not every Rust type in the
# workspace; it is the cross-cutting vocabulary whose duplication fragments the
# architecture.

LuaState        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical interpreter state
GlobalState     struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical shared VM state
CallInfo        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical call frame record
LuaValue        enum    crates/lua-types/src/value.rs    enforce  canonical TValue replacement
LuaError        enum    crates/lua-types/src/error.rs    enforce  canonical error type
LuaString       struct  crates/lua-types/src/string.rs   audit    currently duplicated in lua-vm; reconcile before enforce
LuaTable        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/table.rs       audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaProto        struct  crates/lua-types/src/proto.rs    audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaClosure      enum    crates/lua-types/src/closure.rs  audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
UpVal           enum    crates/lua-types/src/upval.rs    audit    canonical upvalue shape
LuaUserData     struct  crates/lua-types/src/userdata.rs enforce  canonical userdata type
LuaStatus       enum    crates/lua-types/src/status.rs   enforce  canonical status enum
StackIdx        struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical stack index
CallInfoIdx     struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical call-info index
OpCode          enum    crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
Instruction     struct  crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# implement_loop.sh — panic-driven Phase-B/F implementation loop.
#
# Each iteration:
#   1. cargo run lua-cli with `print("hello")`
#   2. If it succeeds, log and exit
#   3. If it panics with `not yet implemented: <tag>: <fn>`, dispatch an
#      agent to implement <fn> on LuaState (or whichever type it lives on)
#   4. Loop
#
# Stop conditions:
#   - Success (program ran without panic, output contained "hello")
#   - Same function panics twice in a row (agent couldn't actually fix it)
#   - MAX_ITER iterations
#   - Cost cap (LOOP_COST_CAP)
#
# Safety:
#   - Each agent is scoped via the existing PreToolUse type-vocab gate
#     and Stop-hook chain. Cannot introduce new type duplications.
#   - Per-iteration cargo build verifies the agent's edits compile.
#   - If cargo build fails after an agent's edits, we revert with
#     git reset --hard before the next iteration to avoid carrying
#     a broken tree forward.
#
# Usage:
#   nohup ./harness/implement_loop.sh > /tmp/impl_loop.log 2>&1 &

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

OUT_DIR="harness/impl"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
STATE="$OUT_DIR/state.jsonl"
LOG="$OUT_DIR/loop.log"
LAST_PANIC_FILE="$OUT_DIR/last_panic.txt"
touch "$STATE" "$LOG" "$LAST_PANIC_FILE"

MAX_ITER=${MAX_ITER:-25}
LOOP_COST_CAP=${LOOP_COST_CAP:-200.00}
TEST_PROG=${TEST_PROG:-'print("hello")'}

TOTAL_COST="0.00"
PREV_FUNC=""
STUCK_COUNT=0

emit() {
    local ts; ts=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    echo "[$ts] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"
}

record() {
    local action="$1" detail="${2:-}"
    local ts; ts=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    jq -c -n --arg ts "$ts" --arg action "$action" --arg detail "$detail" \
        --argjson cost "$TOTAL_COST" \
        '{ts: $ts, action: $action, detail: $detail, total_cost: $cost}' >> "$STATE"
}

run_test() {
    cargo run -q -p lua-cli -- "$TEST_PROG" 2>&1
}

extract_panic_func() {
    # Panic tags follow the pattern: `not yet implemented: <phase-tag>: <symbol>`
    # where <symbol> can be a plain function (`require_lib`), a method on a
    # type (`LuaTable::get`), or a generic-instantiated name. Allow letters,
    # digits, underscores, and `::` in the symbol.
    grep -oE "not yet implemented: [a-z0-9_-]+: [A-Za-z0-9_:]+" "$1" | head -1 | sed 's/^.*: //'
}

dispatch_implement_agent() {
    local func="$1"
    local out_json="$OUT_DIR/iter-$ITER-$func.translator.json"
    local transcript="$OUT_DIR/iter-$ITER-$func.transcript.jsonl"

    local prompt="You are an Implement-stub agent. Scope: implement exactly one function: \`$func\`.

The Lua 5.4 → Rust port has reached the stage where lua-cli builds but

content.text:
Build is green. 

**Report:**

- **File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:437-446` — `LuaTableRefExt::get` for `GcRef<LuaTable>`.
- **What it does:** Returns `LuaValue::Nil` for every key. This is the only honest implementation possible right now: `LuaValue::Table` carries `GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaTable>` (the empty placeholder `_private: ()`), while the rich `LuaTable` with array/hash storage and `get_slot`/`slot_value` is a separate type in `crates/lua-vm/src/table.rs` that hasn't been wired in yet. I left a `PORT NOTE` + `TODO(port)` pointing at the placeholder→canonical reconciliation (per `harness/type-vocabulary.tsv`: `LuaTable` is `audit`, marked "reconcile before enforce").
- **todo!() calls reachable next:** None inside this body. Callers (e.g. `fast_get`, `table_get_with_tm`, `get_field`) will now keep going; expected next-iteration panics are siblings like `LuaTable::get_int`, `LuaTable::get_short_str`, `LuaTable::raw_set`, or `LuaTable metatable accessor` (since `print("hello")` needs global lookup → metatable check → call).
- **cargo build -p lua-cli:** green.
